findings.location: s3 (１件ごとの処理) — SqlRowからFormへの値設定 note block
findings.description: The source contains a `note` admonition with a concrete code example demonstrating how to handle items requiring editing (e.g., date calculation) when constructing from SqlRow. The pattern — create from SqlRow first, then call setters for edited fields — is described in prose but the illustrative code example is missing.
findings.source_evidence: Source note block: `DeleteUserReportTempEntity entity = new DeleteUserReportTempEntity(userInfo)` / `entity.setSampleItem(DateUtil.addDay(userInfo.getString("SampleItem"), 100));` — demonstrates the correct sequence of SqlRow-based construction followed by explicit setter calls for computed fields.

findings.description: `Success` (the return type in `return new Success()` inside the `handle` method) is a PascalCase class name present in the code example but absent from s3 hints.
findings.source_evidence: Source handle method ends with `return new Success();` — `Success` is a concrete class reference used in the implementation.
